edits.description: Replace the bundled arm64-v8a libnativestub.so with a rebuilt binary. ChangeClassifier has no packaging story for a changed native library - it's an UNSUPPORTED_FILE_CHANGED fallback (see ChangeClassifierTest's 'native library under jniLibs falls back honestly'), so this must route to a full Gradle rebaseline rather than be served as a quick build (D7's '.so change routes to REBASELINE').

assertions.name: op_success
assertions.detail: fallback recorded: no daemon op invoked (full Gradle build expected)

edits.description: Change the note label PingReceiver.onReceive() inserts. Body-only edit to a manifest receiver: receivers are NOT in the restart closure (fresh instantiation per delivery via the factory serves current code), so the deploy hot-swaps without a process restart.

edits.description: Decorate every label NotesProvider.query() serves with a marker suffix. Add a new string resource (new_feature_label) and reference it from MainActivity via R.string.new_feature_label. This is the R-id-regeneration case: aapt2 must assign the new resource an ID (not just relink an existing one) before the code that references it can compile, so the route is mixed (relink AND compile), not resources-only.

edits.description: PR-mined (subgraph-extending): modeled after Rosemoe/sora-editor PR #267 'Added more keybindings' (merged 2022-10-18), which added CharPosition's zero-arg/2-arg/3-arg constructor overloads -- those are already baked into our pinned commit (43f7e8e2981...), so a literal forward-patch of PR #267's own diff is a no-op against this baseline. edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 192c9356991df297a0c02f9d1c85a24e4022aacc 'feat(editor): no longer use enforced LTR base direction in editor' (Rosemoe, 2025-11-10), which moved TextBidi.getDirections off a hardcoded Bidi.DIRECTION_LEFT_TO_RIGHT onto Bidi.DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit (TextBidi.java already uses DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit continues the same real spirit ('stop enforcing a single base direction') with the natural next step: an overload that takes an explicit Bidi direction flag, with the existing single-arg method delegating to it.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'quick build reloaded TextBidi.getDirections(text, bidiFlags)' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 10370f833f496363d44a4d76380e29bd1e7e77e2 'perf(editor): avoid copying when create String in Latin1 implementation' (Rosemoe, 2026-03-03), which changed StringLatin1.newString() from a char[]-roundtrip to a direct new String(bytes, offset, length, ISO_8859_1) decode. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit's newString() (visible a few lines below this edit's change), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit applies the SAME real perf pattern to the sibling method appendTo(), which still had the pre-fix per-char loop.
